title: The Weekly Closeout: Another entertainment exec at Gap Inc., and tariff refunds trickle in
description: The apparel conglomerate beefed up its "fashiontainment" roster, while retailers are getting their money back on import levies canceled by the Supreme Court.

title: Department stores losing market share to resale
description: Off-price retailers have little to fear from rapidly growing secondhand sales, though, according to research from Bank of America analysts.

title: Bed Bath & Beyond’s first co-branded store with The Container Store will open Saturday
description: The location in Fort Worth, Texas, comes just over a month after Bed Bath & Beyond announced plans to acquire the organizational retailer.

title: Amazon Business rolls out new credit cards with U.S. Bank and Mastercard
description:
Amazon.com Inc. is updating its business credit card program, shifting new applicants to U.S. Bank and Mastercard as it adds more rewards and financing tools for business buyers. The retailer introduced the new Prime Business Card and Amazon Business Card on May 13. The launch moves new applicants away from American Express, which had issued […]
The post Amazon Business rolls out new credit cards with U.S. Bank and Mastercard appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Parts Town updates PartPredictor with new AI-backed features
description:
Parts Town has upgraded its AI-powered PartPredictor tool with expanded coverage of 120 brands featuring over 18,000 equipment models. The expansion will allow technicians and dispatchers to identify the correct parts faster using everything from model numbers to plain-language symptom descriptions. The distributor — which handles original equipment manufacturer (OEM) parts for food service; heating, […]
The post Parts Town updates PartPredictor with new AI-backed features appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Albertsons uses Google Cloud for new supply chain tool
description:
Albertsons Companies Inc. will deploy a computer vision tool at its distribution centers to help quality inspectors evaluate fresh produce before it ships to stores, it announced on May 13. The grocer said its patent-pending Intelligent Quality Control tool uses artificial intelligence (AI) to assist inspectors, and is already active in some distribution centers. Albertsons […]
The post Albertsons uses Google Cloud for new supply chain tool appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines
description: In a dimly lit bedroom, a frightened young woman is thrown onto a bed by a tall, muscular man. He grabs her hand, and flame-like vines crawl across her body, fusing with her flesh. She levitates, then drops. A dragon-shaped tattoo appears across her chest. “Two months,” the man says. “Give me an heir, or…

title: Garnier taps ‘Love Island’ star to pitch hair ‘moose’ to next generation
description: The L'Oréal brand is using social chatter to tease a new campaign featuring TJ Palma, who misinterprets instructions around promoting hair mousse.

title: Social insights fail to reach decision-makers: Here’s what the numbers say
description: Just 14% of social media professionals feel their organization is able to use social data properly.

title: The AI search shift changing B2B marketing metrics
description:
Website traffic is losing ground as the primary success metric while AI visibility and lead quality take center stage.
The post The AI search shift changing B2B marketing metrics appeared first on MarTech.

title: The art of doing more with less: The new marketing operations stack
description:
Stop managing tools and start orchestrating outcomes. At the May MarTech Conference we discussed untangling the ‘AI spaghetti mess’ to move forward.
The post The art of doing more with less: The new marketing operations stack appeared first on MarTech.

title: Why scaling creative is really a leadership challenge
description:
Automation can increase output, but creative effectiveness depends on operational discipline and strategic clarity.
The post Why scaling creative is really a leadership challenge appeared first on MarTech.

title: Marketplace Briefing: Why Amazon discontinued its AI-powered Rufus chatbot for Alexa shopping agent
description: Amazon replaced its Rufus chatbot with a new AI assistant called Alexa for Shopping this week as AI search becomes a bigger part of its online store.

title: BNPL giants battle at Walmart
description: The retail behemoth swapped buy now, pay later provider Affirm for Klarna last year, but both BNPL companies say Walmart business is good.

title: Dive Deposits: Affirm’s AI stance runs counter to trend
description: While some fintechs are content to do more with less through AI, BNPL platform Affirm simply aims to do more, as its top execs eschew human layoffs in favor of artificial intelligence.
